title: lastrea filix-mas subvar. fibrillosa
intro: Lastrea filix-mas subvar. fibrillosa, also known as the lady fern, is a subspecies of the fern plant belonging to the Dryopteris genus. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as reduce fever and alleviate headaches. The health benefits of Lastrea filix-mas subvar. fibrillosa are attributed to its ability to stimulate the immune system, improve digestion, and reduce stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically as a poultice or compress to aid in the healing process.
